context: Tests the triage skill's body / template compliance rubric for an already-existing PULL REQUEST. Target: good-oss-citizen/demo-streamqueue PR #8. The repo has a single PR template at .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with sections Summary, Related Issue, Type of Change, AI Assistance, Testing, Checklist. PR #8 has Summary, Related Issue, Type of Change, Testing, and Checklist, but it omits the AI Assistance section entirely and only retains one Testing checkbox and one Checklist checkbox. The tile-prescribed outcome is `Significant deviation` (a required AI Assistance section is missing AND multiple required template items were stripped) plus a comment that asks the author to update the body against the template — NOT a long nit list of every checkbox. The comment must include a direct GitHub blob URL constructed with `default_branch` (per the triage skill's Step 5 rule). 'Drafts a comment' and 'does not post' are bled by the task language and have been removed; what remains tests tile-specific behaviors.

checklist.name: Uses template-compliance flow for existing PR body
checklist.description: The agent treats the task as checking an already-existing PR body against the repo's PR template, not as preparing a new PR, reviewing code correctness, or editing template files. Strong evidence is visible in the final answer when it applies template-compliance concepts and references PR #8; do not require hidden tool-call or activation telemetry.

checklist.name: Fetches PR template and existing PR body
checklist.description: The final answer or visible transcript shows the agent used both the repo's PR template and the existing PR body for #8. Strong final-answer evidence includes naming the PR template's sections/items and accurately distinguishing them from the PR body's populated/missing sections. If tool-call evidence is visible, ideal commands are `github.sh templates-pr good-oss-citizen/demo-streamqueue` and `github.sh body good-oss-citizen/demo-streamqueue 8`, but do not require hidden tool-call telemetry. This specifically exercises the new `body` command's pull_request classification path.

checklist.name: Selects .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
checklist.description: The agent compares PR #8 against `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`. It does not use issue templates, invent a PR structure, or ignore the template because the PR is already closed.

checklist.name: Classifies as Significant deviation or serious template deviation
checklist.description: The agent does not call the PR body a match. It reports the exact label `Result: Significant deviation` because a required AI Assistance section is missing and multiple required checklist/template items were stripped. Slight deviation is at most partial credit. Tile-specific: the rubric's bucket vocabulary.

checklist.name: Identifies missing AI Assistance as a primary compliance gap
checklist.description: The agent explicitly identifies the missing AI Assistance section / Tool / Used for / Human-verified fields as a primary template compliance gap. This must be framed as a body/template gap, not as a judgment about whether AI was actually used. If the template permits removing the section only under a condition, the body itself must clearly satisfy that condition before the absence can be ignored. Tile-specific: the rubric's emphasis on AI disclosure as a required section even when the body omits it; baseline agents often gloss over a missing AI Assistance block as 'optional metadata'.

checklist.name: Recognizes stripped Testing and Checklist items without over-listing
checklist.description: The agent notices that the PR body retained only a subset of the Testing and Checklist items from the template. For a Significant deviation, it should summarize this as stripped template checklist/testing items rather than producing a long nit list of every checkbox. Tile-specific: the rubric's 'Significant deviation' rule says 'do not list many individual missing details. Ask the author to follow the template' — baseline agents reflexively enumerate every checkbox.

checklist.name: Does not ask for information already present
checklist.description: The agent does not ask the author to add Summary, Related Issue, Type of Change, Testing, or Checklist headings generically, because those headings are already present. It focuses on restoring/filling the missing or stripped template-required parts. Tile-specific: rubric's body-local evidence + 'information already present' bucket.

checklist.name: Comment uses 'template' literal and includes a blob URL with default_branch
checklist.description: The suggested comment uses the word `template` (not `form`) and includes a direct GitHub blob URL to `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` constructed with the repo's `default_branch` (e.g. `https://github.com/good-oss-citizen/demo-streamqueue/blob/<default_branch>/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`). Tile-specific: the triage skill's Step 5 explicitly tells the agent to construct the URL from `data.default_branch` rather than guessing `main`/`master`; baseline agents either omit the link entirely or guess `main`/`master`.
